description: baseline (SwiGLU d16, partial RoPE, value embeddings, token shifting, QK-norm, SSSL window pattern, Muon+AdamW, WSD 5% warmup + 95% stable, muP base=768, MXFP8, 222M params, 200M tokens on ClimbMix)

Differential attention: pair 6 heads into 3 diff heads (y1-sigmoid(lambda)*y2 with lambda init 0.8) + head RMSNorm + c_proj 384->768 (hypothesis: subtracting paired attention outputs cancels noise per ICLR2025 paper arxiv:2410.05258; evidence: modded-nanogpt community gains): val_bpb 0.919->0.925 WORSE. Conclusion: halving attention output dim from 768 to 384 before c_proj reduces model capacity more than noise cancellation helps. Simple output-pairing version of diff attention loses too much expressivity at 6-head scale. Need full formulation (4 SDPA calls to preserve output dim) or more heads.

Remove MLP-only layers: set MLP_ONLY_LAYERS={} so all 16 layers have attention+MLP (hypothesis: 3 MLP-only layers waste capacity since throughput irrelevant with fixed token budget; evidence: 3.9GB unused VRAM): val_bpb 0.919->0.923 WORSE. Added 7M params (222->229M) and 3 more attention layers (all short-window S-layers). Conclusion: baseline attention/MLP balance was already optimal at this depth. Short-window attention in layers 12-14 adds compute without quality benefit. MLP-only layers were NOT a bottleneck.
